1â†’package api
     2â†’
     3â†’import (
     4â†’	"net/http"
     5â†’
     6â†’	"github.com/jackc/pgx/v5/pgxpool"
     7â†’	"github.com/sentioxyz/changelogue/internal/ingestion"
     8â†’	"github.com/sentioxyz/changelogue/internal/routing"
     9â†’)
    10â†’
    11â†’// Dependencies holds all external dependencies required for registering API routes.
    12â†’type Dependencies struct {
    13â†’	DB                    *pgxpool.Pool
    14â†’	ProjectsStore         ProjectsStore
    15â†’	ReleasesStore         ReleasesStore
    16â†’	SubscriptionsStore    SubscriptionsStore
    17â†’	SourcesStore          SourcesStore
    18â†’	ChannelsStore         ChannelsStore
    19â†’	ContextSourcesStore   ContextSourcesStore
    20â†’	SemanticReleasesStore SemanticReleasesStore
    21â†’	AgentStore            AgentStore
    22â†’	KeyStore              KeyStore
    23â†’	HealthChecker         HealthChecker
    24â†’	Broadcaster           *Broadcaster
    25â†’	NoAuth                bool
    26â†’	IngestionService      *ingestion.Service
    27â†’	HTTPClient            *http.Client
    28â†’}
    29â†’
    30â†’// RegisterRoutes registers all API v1 routes on the given ServeMux.
    31â†’func RegisterRoutes(mux *http.ServeMux, deps Dependencies) {
    32â†’	// Public chain: no auth required.
    33â†’	publicChain := Chain(RequestID, Logger, Recovery)
    34â†’
    35â†’	// Authenticated chain: includes auth unless NoAuth is set.
    36â†’	chain := publicChain
    37â†’	if !deps.NoAuth {
    38â†’		chain = Chain(RequestID, Logger, Recovery, RateLimit(10, 20), Auth(deps.KeyStore))
    39â†’	}
    40â†’
    41â†’	// Projects (CRUD)
    42â†’	projects := NewProjectsHandler(deps.ProjectsStore)
    43â†’	mux.Handle("GET /api/v1/projects", chain(http.HandlerFunc(projects.List)))
    44â†’	mux.Handle("POST /api/v1/projects", chain(http.HandlerFunc(projects.Create)))
    45â†’	mux.Handle("GET /api/v1/projects/{id}", chain(http.HandlerFunc(projects.Get)))
    46â†’	mux.Handle("PUT /api/v1/projects/{id}", chain(http.HandlerFunc(projects.Update)))
    47â†’	mux.Handle("DELETE /api/v1/projects/{id}", chain(http.HandlerFunc(projects.Delete)))
    48â†’
    49â†’	// Sources (nested under projects)
    50â†’	sources := NewSourcesHandler(deps.SourcesStore, deps.IngestionService, deps.HTTPClient)
    51â†’	mux.Handle("GET /api/v1/projects/{projectId}/sources", chain(http.HandlerFunc(sources.List)))
    52â†’	mux.Handle("POST /api/v1/projects/{projectId}/sources", chain(http.HandlerFunc(sources.Create)))
    53â†’	mux.Handle("GET /api/v1/sources/{id}", chain(http.HandlerFunc(sources.Get)))
    54â†’	mux.Handle("PUT /api/v1/sources/{id}", chain(http.HandlerFunc(sources.Update)))
    55â†’	mux.Handle("DELETE /api/v1/sources/{id}", chain(http.HandlerFunc(sources.Delete)))
    56â†’	mux.Handle("POST /api/v1/sources/{id}/poll", chain(http.HandlerFunc(sources.FetchReleases)))
    57â†’
    58â†’	// Context Sources (nested under projects)
    59â†’	contextSources := NewContextSourcesHandler(deps.ContextSourcesStore)
    60â†’	mux.Handle("GET /api/v1/projects/{projectId}/context-sources", chain(http.HandlerFunc(contextSources.List)))
    61â†’	mux.Handle("POST /api/v1/projects/{projectId}/context-sources", chain(http.HandlerFunc(contextSources.Create)))
    62â†’	mux.Handle("GET /api/v1/context-sources/{id}", chain(http.HandlerFunc(contextSources.Get)))
    63â†’	mux.Handle("PUT /api/v1/context-sources/{id}", chain(http.HandlerFunc(contextSources.Update)))
    64â†’	mux.Handle("DELETE /api/v1/context-sources/{id}", chain(http.HandlerFunc(contextSources.Delete)))
    65â†’
    66â†’	// Releases (read-only, nested under sources and projects)
    67â†’	releases := NewReleasesHandler(deps.ReleasesStore)
    68â†’	mux.Handle("GET /api/v1/releases", chain(http.HandlerFunc(releases.List)))
    69â†’	mux.Handle("GET /api/v1/sources/{id}/releases", chain(http.HandlerFunc(releases.ListBySource)))
    70â†’	mux.Handle("GET /api/v1/projects/{projectId}/releases", chain(http.HandlerFunc(releases.ListByProject)))
    71â†’	mux.Handle("GET /api/v1/releases/{id}", chain(http.HandlerFunc(releases.Get)))
    72â†’
    73â†’	// Semantic Releases (nested under projects + top-level list)
    74â†’	semanticReleases := NewSemanticReleasesHandler(deps.SemanticReleasesStore)
    75â†’	mux.Handle("GET /api/v1/semantic-releases", chain(http.HandlerFunc(semanticReleases.ListAll)))
    76â†’	mux.Handle("GET /api/v1/projects/{projectId}/semantic-releases", chain(http.HandlerFunc(semanticReleases.List)))
    77â†’	mux.Handle("GET /api/v1/semantic-releases/{id}", chain(http.HandlerFunc(semanticReleases.Get)))
    78â†’	mux.Handle("DELETE /api/v1/semantic-releases/{id}", chain(http.HandlerFunc(semanticReleases.Delete)))
    79â†’
    80â†’	// Subscriptions (CRUD)
    81â†’	subscriptions := NewSubscriptionsHandler(deps.SubscriptionsStore)
    82â†’	mux.Handle("GET /api/v1/subscriptions", chain(http.HandlerFunc(subscriptions.List)))
    83â†’	mux.Handle("POST /api/v1/subscriptions", chain(http.HandlerFunc(subscriptions.Create)))
    84â†’	mux.Handle("GET /api/v1/subscriptions/{id}", chain(http.HandlerFunc(subscriptions.Get)))
    85â†’	mux.Handle("PUT /api/v1/subscriptions/{id}", chain(http.HandlerFunc(subscriptions.Update)))
    86â†’	mux.Handle("DELETE /api/v1/subscriptions/{id}", chain(http.HandlerFunc(subscriptions.Delete)))
    87â†’
    88â†’	// Channels (CRUD + test)
    89â†’	channels := NewChannelsHandler(deps.ChannelsStore, routing.NewSenders())
    90â†’	mux.Handle("GET /api/v1/channels", chain(http.HandlerFunc(channels.List)))
    91â†’	mux.Handle("POST /api/v1/channels", chain(http.HandlerFunc(channels.Create)))
    92â†’	mux.Handle("GET /api/v1/channels/{id}", chain(http.HandlerFunc(channels.Get)))
    93â†’	mux.Handle("PUT /api/v1/channels/{id}", chain(http.HandlerFunc(channels.Update)))
    94â†’	mux.Handle("DELETE /api/v1/channels/{id}", chain(http.HandlerFunc(channels.Delete)))
    95â†’	mux.Handle("POST /api/v1/channels/{id}/test", chain(http.HandlerFunc(channels.Test)))
    96â†’
    97â†’	// Agent
    98â†’	agent := NewAgentHandler(deps.AgentStore)
    99â†’	mux.Handle("POST /api/v1/projects/{projectId}/agent/run", chain(http.HandlerFunc(agent.TriggerRun)))
   100â†’	mux.Handle("GET /api/v1/projects/{projectId}/agent/runs", chain(http.HandlerFunc(agent.ListRuns)))
   101â†’	mux.Handle("GET /api/v1/agent-runs/{id}", chain(http.HandlerFunc(agent.GetRun)))
   102â†’
   103â†’	// Providers (metadata â€” static, no store needed)
   104â†’	providers := NewProvidersHandler()
   105â†’	mux.Handle("GET /api/v1/providers", chain(http.HandlerFunc(providers.List)))
   106â†’
   107â†’	// SSE events
   108â†’	events := NewEventsHandler(deps.Broadcaster)
   109â†’	mux.Handle("GET /api/v1/events", chain(http.HandlerFunc(events.Stream)))
   110â†’
   111â†’	// Health (public â€” no auth middleware)
   112â†’	health := NewHealthHandler(deps.HealthChecker)
   113â†’	mux.Handle("GET /api/v1/health", publicChain(http.HandlerFunc(health.Check)))
   114â†’	mux.Handle("GET /api/v1/stats", chain(http.HandlerFunc(health.Stats)))
   115â†’	mux.Handle("GET /api/v1/stats/trend", chain(http.HandlerFunc(health.Trend)))
   116â†’}
   117â†’

All done. Here is a summary of what was implemented:

### Changes made

**`/Users/pc/web3/ReleaseBeacon/internal/api/semantic_releases.go`** -- Added the `ListSources` handler method on `SemanticReleasesHandler`. It extracts the `{id}` path parameter, calls `store.GetSemanticReleaseSources()`, normalizes a nil slice to an empty array, and returns the result as JSON with status 200.

**`/Users/pc/web3/ReleaseBeacon/internal/api/server.go`** -- Registered the new route `GET /api/v1/semantic-releases/{id}/sources` with the authenticated middleware chain, placed before the existing DELETE route.

**`/Users/pc/web3/ReleaseBeacon/internal/api/semantic_releases_test.go`** -- Added the new route to `setupSemanticReleasesMux` and added two tests:
- `TestSemanticReleasesHandlerListSources` -- verifies the endpoint returns 2 source releases when the mock store has data.
- `TestSemanticReleasesHandlerListSourcesEmpty` -- verifies the endpoint returns an empty JSON array `[]` (not `null`) when no sources exist.

All 10 semantic releases tests pass. Commit `44346ad` created on branch `main`.

    24â†’async function request<T>(path: string, init?: RequestInit): Promise<T> {
    25â†’  const res = await fetch(`${BASE}${path}`, {
    26â†’    headers: { "Content-Type": "application/json", ...init?.headers },
    27â†’    ...init,
    28â†’  });
    29â†’  if (!res.ok) {
    30â†’    const body = await res.json().catch(() => null);
    31â†’    throw new Error(body?.error?.message ?? `Request failed: ${res.status}`);
    32â†’  }
    33â†’  if (res.status === 204) return null as T;
    34â†’  return res.json();
    35â†’}

    51â†’      method: "PUT",
    52â†’      body: JSON.stringify(input),
    53â†’    }),
    54â†’  delete: (id: string) =>
    55â†’    request<ApiResponse<null>>(`/projects/${id}`, { method: "DELETE" }),
    56â†’};

    62â†’    request<ApiResponse<Source[]>>(`/projects/${projectId}/sources?page=${page}`),
    63â†’  create: (projectId: string, input: SourceInput) =>

    75â†’  delete: (id: string) =>

    77â†’  poll: (id: string) =>
    78â†’    request<ApiResponse<{ new_releases: number }>>(`/sources/${id}/poll`, {
    79â†’      method: "POST",
    80â†’    }),
    81â†’};
    82â†’
    83â†’export const releases = {
    84â†’  list: (page = 1, perPage = 25) =>
    85â†’    request<ApiResponse<Release[]>>(`/releases?page=${page}&per_page=${perPage}`),
    86â†’  listBySource: (sourceId: string, page = 1) =>

    88â†’  listByProject: (projectId: string, page = 1, perPage = 25) =>
    89â†’    request<ApiResponse<Release[]>>(`/projects/${projectId}/releases?page=${page}&per_page=${perPage}`),

    98â†’    request<ApiResponse<ContextSource[]>>(`/projects/${projectId}/context-sources?page=${page}`),
    99â†’  create: (projectId: string, input: ContextSourceInput) =>
   100â†’    request<ApiResponse<ContextSource>>(`/projects/${projectId}/context-sources`, {

   120â†’  list: (projectId: string, page = 1, perPage = 25) =>

You are implementing Task 4: Rewrite the semantic release detail component. This is the main frontend task. ## File to modify `web/components/semantic-releases/semantic-release-detail.tsx` â€” full rewrite ## Available imports and their APIs **API Client** (`@/lib/api/client`): - `semanticReleases as srApi` â€” `.get(id)`, `.delete(id)`, `.getSources(id)` (NEW) - `projects as projectsApi` â€” `.get(id)` - `sources as sourcesApi` â€” `.listByProject(projectId)` - `contextSources` â€” `.list(projectId)` â€” returns `ApiResponse` **Types** (`@/lib/api/types`): ```typescript interface SemanticRelease { id: string; project_id: string; version: string; report?: SemanticReport; status: string; error?: string; created_at: string; completed_at?: string; } interface SemanticReport { subject?: string; risk_level?: string; risk_reason?: string; status_checks?: string[]; changelog_summary?: string; download_commands?: string[]; download_links?: string[]; summary?: string; availability?: string; adoption?: string; urgency?: string; recommendation?: string; } interface ContextSource { id: string; project_id: string; type: string; name: string; config: Record; created_at: string; updated_at: string; } interface Source { id: string; project_id: string; provider: string; repository: string; poll_interval_seconds: number; enabled: boolean; config?: Record; last_polled_at?: string; last_error?: string; created_at: string; updated_at: string; } interface Release { id: string; source_id: string; version: string; raw_data?: Record; released_at?: string; created_at: string; } ``` **UI Components available:** - `StatusDot` from `@/components/ui/status-dot` â€” `` - `VersionChip` from `@/components/ui/version-chip` â€” `` - `SectionLabel` from `@/components/ui/section-label` â€” `Label` â€” renders uppercase 11px gray label - `ProviderBadge` from `@/components/ui/provider-badge` â€” `` â€” GitHub/Docker badges - `timeAgo` from `@/lib/format` â€” `timeAgo(dateStr)` â€” returns "5m ago", "2h ago", "3d ago" **Icons:** Use `lucide-react` â€” available icons include: `ArrowLeft`, `Check`, `ExternalLink`, `Copy`, `AlertTriangle`, `AlertCircle`, `Shield`, `ShieldAlert`, `BookOpen`, `Link2` ## Design specification The component layout from top to bottom: ### 1. Back link (unchanged) Link to `/semantic-releases` with ArrowLeft icon. ### 2. Project byline (unchanged) Project name in italic Fraunces 13px, gray. ### 3. Version heading (unchanged) `sr.version` at 42px bold Fraunces. ### 4. Subject line (NEW) If `sr.report?.subject`, render it as: ```tsx

{sr.report.subject}

``` ### 5. Meta line (unchanged) StatusDot + status text + age + delete button. ### 6. Divider (unchanged) ### 7. Error state (unchanged) ### 8. Risk & Urgency Banner (NEW â€” replaces old UrgencyCallout) Show if `sr.report.risk_level` or `sr.report.urgency` exists. Color mapping by `risk_level` (uppercase): - CRITICAL: border `#dc2626`, bg `#fff1f2`, text `#991b1b` - HIGH: border `#d97706`, bg `#fff8f0`, text `#92400e` - MEDIUM: border `#ca8a04`, bg `#fefce8`, text `#854d0e` - LOW/default: border `#16a34a`, bg `#f0fdf4`, text `#166534` Layout: ```tsx
{riskLevel} RISK {urgency && ( {urgency} Urgency )}
{risk_reason && (

{sr.report.risk_reason}

)}
``` ### 9. Status Checks & Downloads (NEW) Show if any of `status_checks`, `download_links`, or `download_commands` are non-empty. ```tsx
Availability & Downloads {/* Status checks as green pills */} {status_checks?.length > 0 && (
{status_checks.map(check => ( {check} ))}
)} {/* Download links as pills */} {download_links?.length > 0 && (
{download_links.map(link => ( {/* Extract domain from URL for display */} {new URL(link).hostname.replace('www.', '')} ))}
)} {/* Download commands as copiable code */} {download_commands?.length > 0 && (
{download_commands.map(cmd => (
{cmd}
))}
)}
``` ### 10. Adoption (conditional) Only show if `sr.report.adoption` exists. ```tsx {sr.report.adoption && (
Adoption

{sr.report.adoption}

)} ``` ### 11. Source Releases (FIXED â€” uses getSources) Fetch with: `useSWR(`sr-sources-${srId}`, () => srApi.getSources(srId))` Same table layout as before but: - Data comes from `srApi.getSources(srId)` instead of `releasesApi.listByProject(projectId)` - Still needs `sourcesApi.listByProject(projectId)` for provider badges/repo names - Version chips are clickable links: - For GitHub: `https://github.com/{repository}/releases/tag/{version}` - For Docker Hub: `https://hub.docker.com/r/{repository}/tags?name={version}` - Otherwise: no link, just a VersionChip Make version clickable: ```tsx const versionUrl = source?.provider === 'github' ? `https://github.com/${source.repository}/releases/tag/${rel.version}` : source?.provider === 'dockerhub' ? `https://hub.docker.com/r/${source.repository}/tags?name=${rel.version}` : null; // In the Version column: {versionUrl ? ( ) : ( )} ``` ### 12. Context Sources (NEW) Fetch with: `useSWR(`project-context-sources-${projectId}`, () => contextSources.list(projectId))` Only show if context sources exist: ```tsx {contextSourcesList.length > 0 && (
Context Sources
{contextSourcesList.map(cs => { const url = cs.config?.url as string | undefined; return (
{cs.name} {cs.type}
{url && ( )}
); })}
)} ``` ### 13. Changelog Summary (RENAMED) If `sr.report.changelog_summary` exists: ```tsx {sr.report.changelog_summary && (
Changelog Summary

{sr.report.changelog_summary}

)} ``` ### 14. Recommendation (unchanged) Pull-quote blockquote with orange left border, Fraunces italic. ## Data fetching changes Remove: - `import { releases as releasesApi } from "@/lib/api/client"` â€” no longer needed - The SWR call for `releasesApi.listByProject(projectId)` Add: - `import { contextSources } from "@/lib/api/client"` - SWR call: `useSWR(`sr-sources-${srId}`, () => srApi.getSources(srId))` - SWR call: `useSWR(`project-context-sources-${projectId}`, () => contextSources.list(projectId))` Remove the old UrgencyCallout import â€” we handle risk/urgency inline now. ## Important notes - Do NOT remove the `fade-in` class from the container - Keep `max-w-[760px]` container width - Keep existing font patterns (Fraunces for display, DM Sans for body, JetBrains Mono for code) - All sections inside `sr.report && (...)` should be in a `
` - Wrap the download link hostname extraction in a try-catch in case of malformed URLs - The component is `"use client"` â€” keep that ## After writing 1. Write the complete file using the Write tool 2. Run `cd /Users/pc/web3/ReleaseBeacon/web && npx next build` to verify compilation 3. Commit: `git commit -m "feat(web): redesign semantic release detail page with full report fields"` Write the complete rewritten component now.

The semantic release detail component has been fully rewritten and the build passes. Here is a summary of all changes made to `/Users/pc/web3/ReleaseBeacon/web/components/semantic-releases/semantic-release-detail.tsx`:

### Imports changed
- **Removed**: `releases as releasesApi` from the API client, `UrgencyCallout` component
- **Added**: `contextSources` from the API client, `Check`, `ExternalLink`, `Copy`, `ShieldAlert`, `BookOpen` icons from `lucide-react`

### Data fetching changed
- **Removed**: `useSWR` call to `releasesApi.listByProject(projectId)` for all project releases
- **Added**: `useSWR('sr-sources-${srId}', () => srApi.getSources(srId))` -- fetches only the source releases linked to this semantic release
- **Added**: `useSWR('project-context-sources-${projectId}', () => contextSources.list(projectId))` -- fetches context sources for the project

### New helper functions
- `getRiskColors(riskLevel)` -- maps CRITICAL/HIGH/MEDIUM/LOW to border, background, and text colors
- `safeHostname(url)` -- extracts hostname from URL with try-catch for malformed URLs

### New sections (in order)
1. **Subject line** (section 4) -- renders `sr.report.subject` at 20px DM Sans below the version heading
2. **Risk & Urgency Banner** (section 8) -- replaces the old `UrgencyCallout` with a color-coded banner showing risk level badge, urgency text, and risk reason
3. **Availability & Downloads** (section 9) -- three sub-sections:
   - Status checks as green pills with check icons
   - Download links as clickable pills showing extracted hostnames
   - Download commands as copiable code blocks with copy-on-hover buttons
4. **Adoption** (section 10) -- conditional card showing adoption info
5. **Source Releases** (section 11) -- same table but now with clickable version chips linking to GitHub releases or Docker Hub tags
6. **Context Sources** (section 12) -- lists context sources with BookOpen icons, type badges, and external link buttons
7. **Changelog Summary** (section 13) -- renamed section rendering `changelog_summary`

### Preserved
- `fade-in` class on container
- `max-w-[760px]` container width
- Font patterns (Fraunces display, DM Sans body, JetBrains Mono code)
- `space-y-10` wrapper for all report sections
- `"use client"` directive
- Back link, project byline, version heading, meta line, divider, error state, and recommendation blockquote

input.prompt:
You are a spec compliance reviewer. Your job is to verify that the implementation matches the spec exactly â€” nothing missing, nothing extra.

## Spec (approved design)

The semantic release detail page should display these sections in order:
1. Back link to /semantic-releases
2. Project byline (italic Fraunces)
3. Version heading (42px bold)
4. Subject line from `report.subject` (20px DM Sans)
5. Meta line (status dot + age + delete)
6. Divider
7. Error state (if applicable)
8. Risk & Urgency Banner â€” color-coded by risk_level (CRITICAL=red, HIGH=orange, MEDIUM=yellow, LOW=green), shows risk level badge + urgency + risk_reason
9. Status Checks & Downloads â€” status check pills (green), download links (clickable with domain display), download commands (copiable code)
10. Adoption â€” only shown if present, as a card
11. Source Releases â€” from `srApi.getSources(srId)` (NOT all project releases), version chips clickable (GitHub â†’ releases/tag/, Docker Hub â†’ tags?name=)
12. Context Sources â€” from `contextSources.list(projectId)`, shown if exist, with type badge and link
13. Changelog Summary â€” prose text from `changelog_summary`
14. Recommendation â€” pull-quote blockquote with orange left border

## Data fetching requirements
- `srApi.get(srId)` â€” semantic release
- `projectsApi.get(projectId)` â€” project metadata
- `srApi.getSources(srId)` â€” linked source releases (NOT `releasesApi.listByProject`)
- `sourcesApi.listByProject(projectId)` â€” source definitions
- `contextSources.list(projectId)` â€” context sources

## What to check

Read the file `/Users/pc/web3/ReleaseBeacon/web/components/semantic-releases/semantic-release-detail.tsx` and verify:

1. **All sections present** â€” every section from the spec exists in the right order
2. **No missing fields** â€” subject, risk_level, risk_reason, status_checks, download_links, download_commands, changelog_summary are all used
3. **No extra features** â€” nothing added that wasn't in the spec
4. **Data fetching correct** â€” uses getSources not listByProject for releases, fetches context sources
5. **Clickable versions** â€” GitHub and Docker Hub links constructed correctly
6. **Copy functionality** â€” download commands have navigator.clipboard.writeText
7. **Conditional rendering** â€” sections only shown when data exists
8. **UrgencyCallout removed** â€” replaced by the risk/urgency banner

Report your findings as:
- âś… Requirement met (with brief note)
- âťŚ Requirement not met (with what's wrong)

Be thorough. Read the entire file carefully.

    22â†’const BASE = process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_API_URL || "/api/v1";
    23â†’
    24â†’async function request<T>(path: string, init?: RequestInit): Promise<T> {
    25â†’  const res = await fetch(`${BASE}${path}`, {
    26â†’    headers: { "Content-Type": "application/json", ...init?.headers },
    27â†’    ...init,
    28â†’  });
    29â†’  if (!res.ok) {
    30â†’    const body = await res.json().catch(() => null);
    31â†’    throw new Error(body?.error?.message ?? `Request failed: ${res.status}`);
    32â†’  }
    33â†’  if (res.status === 204) return null as T;
    34â†’  return res.json();
    35â†’}

    53â†’    }),

    63â†’  create: (projectId: string, input: SourceInput) =>
    64â†’    request<ApiResponse<Source>>(`/projects/${projectId}/sources`, {

    79â†’      method: "POST",

content.text:
I now have a comprehensive understanding of all the files. Here is the full review. --- # Code Quality Review: Semantic Release Detail Rewrite ## Overall Assessment This is a well-structured, clean implementation. The component reads clearly from top to bottom, the section numbering in comments aids navigation, the UI primitives (`StatusDot`, `VersionChip`, `SectionLabel`, `ProviderBadge`) are appropriately reused, and the styling approach (CSS variables for fonts, Tailwind for layout, hex colors for design tokens) matches the established codebase pattern. The backend handler and tests are solid. Below are the specific findings. --- ## Strengths - **Clean component structure**: The 14-section numbered layout in `/Users/pc/web3/ReleaseBeacon/web/components/semantic-releases/semantic-release-detail.tsx` is easy to navigate and mirrors the spec. - **Good UI primitive reuse**: `StatusDot`, `VersionChip`, `SectionLabel`, and `ProviderBadge` are all shared components, avoiding duplication. - **Defensive `safeHostname` helper** (line 40-46): Gracefully handles malformed URLs with a try/catch. - **Null coalescing throughout**: Arrays default to `[]` via `?? []` on lines 99-102, preventing crashes on missing data. - **Backend handler consistency**: `ListSources` at `/Users/pc/web3/ReleaseBeacon/internal/api/semantic_releases.go` follows the exact same pattern as `Get` and `Delete` (input validation, error wrapping, nil-to-empty-slice normalization). - **Thorough test coverage**: `/Users/pc/web3/ReleaseBeacon/internal/api/semantic_releases_test.go` includes happy path, empty array, error, not-found, and cross-project scenarios for `ListSources`. - **Consistent type definitions**: `/Users/pc/web3/ReleaseBeacon/web/lib/api/types.ts` has all fields marked with proper optionality. --- ## Issues ### Important (should fix) **1. Delete has no error handling -- silent failure on network error** File: `/Users/pc/web3/ReleaseBeacon/web/components/semantic-releases/semantic-release-detail.tsx`, lines 104-108. ```typescript const handleDelete = async () => { if (!confirm("Delete this semantic release?")) return; await srApi.delete(srId); router.push("/semantic-releases"); }; ``` If `srApi.delete(srId)` throws (network error, 404, 500), the promise rejection is unhandled. The user sees nothing, and `router.push` never fires. Meanwhile the `request()` helper in `client.ts` (line 31) throws an `Error` on non-2xx responses, so this is a real path. Compare with the existing `ConfirmDialog` pattern in `/Users/pc/web3/ReleaseBeacon/web/components/ui/confirm-dialog.tsx` which wraps the async operation in try/finally. At minimum, wrap in try/catch and show an alert or toast on failure. **2. `confirm()` used instead of the project's `ConfirmDialog` component** File: `/Users/pc/web3/ReleaseBeacon/web/components/semantic-releases/semantic-release-detail.tsx`, line 105. The codebase has a purpose-built `ConfirmDialog` component at `/Users/pc/web3/ReleaseBeacon/web/components/ui/confirm-dialog.tsx` with loading states and proper accessibility (dialog role, focus management). Using `window.confirm()` is inconsistent with the project's UI patterns and is inaccessible to screen readers in a controlled way. **3. Docker Hub version not URL-encoded in source releases table** File: `/Users/pc/web3/ReleaseBeacon/web/components/semantic-releases/semantic-release-detail.tsx`, lines 413-414. ```typescript ? `https://hub.docker.com/r/${source.repository}/tags?name=${rel.version}` ``` The existing `release-detail.tsx` (line 31) correctly uses `encodeURIComponent(version)` for the Docker Hub URL: ```go return `https://hub.docker.com/r/${repository}/tags?name=${encodeURIComponent(version)}`; ``` Versions like `1.0+build.1` or tags with special characters will produce broken URLs without encoding. **4. `ListSources` handler conflates "not found" with all errors** File: `/Users/pc/web3/ReleaseBeacon/internal/api/semantic_releases.go`, lines 84-86. ```go releases, err := h.store.GetSemanticReleaseSources(r.Context(), id) if err != nil { RespondError(w, r, http.StatusNotFound, "not_found", "Semantic release not found") ``` Any store error (database timeout, connection failure) returns 404. This is the same pattern used by `Get` and `Delete` in this file, so it is locally consistent, but it masks operational errors. A database failure should return 500, not 404. This applies to the `Get` handler as well (line 70-72) but was pre-existing. --- ### Minor (nice to have) **5. Five parallel SWR fetches on mount -- no conditional fetching for secondary data** File: `/Users/pc/web3/ReleaseBeacon/web/components/semantic-releases/semantic-release-detail.tsx`, lines 56-69. All five fetches (`sr`, `project`, `srSources`, `sources`, `contextSources`) fire immediately on mount. The `sourcesData` and `contextSourcesData` are only consumed inside the report sections, and `srSourcesData` is only used if the report exists. None of these are conditioned on `data` being available first. The user sees nothing, and `router.push` never fires. Meanwhile the `request()` helper in `client.ts` (line 31) throws an `Error` on non-2xx responses, so this is a real path. Compare with the existing `ConfirmDialog` pattern in `/Users/pc/web3/ReleaseBeacon/web/components/ui/confirm-dialog.tsx` which wraps the async operation in try/finally. At minimum, wrap in try/catch and show an alert or toast on failure. **2. `confirm()` used instead of the project's `ConfirmDialog` component** File: `/Users/pc/web3/ReleaseBeacon/web/components/semantic-releases/semantic-release-detail.tsx`, line 105. Using `window.confirm()` is inconsistent with the project's UI patterns and is inaccessible to screen readers in a controlled way. **3. Docker Hub version not URL-encoded in source releases table** File: `/Users/pc/web3/ReleaseBeacon/web/components/semantic-releases/semantic-release-detail.tsx`, lines 413-414. ```typescript ? `https://hub.docker.com/r/${source.repository}/tags?name=${rel.version}` ``` The existing `release-detail.tsx` (line 31) correctly uses `encodeURIComponent(version)` for the Docker Hub URL: ```go return `https://hub.docker.com/r/${repository}/tags?name=${encodeURIComponent(version)}`; ``` Versions like `1.0+build.1` or tags with special characters will produce broken URLs without encoding. **4. `ListSources` handler conflates "not found" with all errors** File: `/Users/pc/web3/ReleaseBeacon/internal/api/semantic_releases.go`, lines 84-86. ```go releases, err := h.store.GetSemanticReleaseSources(r.Context(), id) if err != nil { RespondError(w, r, http.StatusNotFound, "not_found", "Semantic release not found") ``` Any store error (database timeout, connection failure) returns 404. This session is being continued from a previous conversation that ran out of context. The summary below covers the earlier portion of the conversation.

Analysis:
Let me chronologically analyze the conversation:

1. **User's initial request**: Redesign the semantic release detail page. The user provided a concrete example of the full data model (go-ethereum v1.16.7) showing fields like subject, urgency, risk_level, risk_reason, status_checks, download_links, download_commands, changelog_summary, etc. The user noted that the current page at `/projects/f7faca53-148e-4e87-b19c-c31ddeca389e/semantic-releases/57084bf4-6cb7-4d49-b99a-25e429995b73` doesn't show all this information. They specified the desired layout: highlight docker image/binary available with download links, risk level, urgency, adoption (optional), related source versions (clickable), context source links, and a summary.

2. **Brainstorming phase**: Used brainstorming skill, explored the codebase thoroughly:
   - Found the frontend TypeScript `SemanticReport` interface only has 5 fields (summary, availability, adoption, urgency, recommendation)
   - The Go backend `SemanticReport` has 12 fields including subject, risk_level, risk_reason, status_checks, changelog_summary, download_commands, download_links
   - The detail page showed ALL project releases instead of linked ones
   - Asked user about adding a new API endpoint for linked source releases â†’ user approved "Yes, add new endpoint"

3. **Design proposal**: Proposed an "Information-Dense Briefing Layout" with 9 sections. User approved with "Looks good".

4. **Implementation plan**: Created a 6-task implementation plan saved to `docs/plans/2026-02-28-semantic-release-detail-redesign.md`. User chose "Subagent-Driven" execution (option 1).

5. **Task 1 (Backend)**: Added `ListSources` handler to `semantic_releases.go`, registered route in `server.go`, added tests. Committed as `44346ad`.

6. **Tasks 2-3 (Frontend types + client)**: Updated `SemanticReport` interface with all 12 fields (all optional), added `getSources` method to API client. Committed as `5d84eb6` and `eaf3435`.

7. **Task 4 (Component rewrite)**: Full rewrite of `semantic-release-detail.tsx`. Spec review passed 100%. Code quality review found issues:
   - Important: Delete has no error handling (fixed with try/catch)
   - Important: Docker Hub URL not encoded (fixed with encodeURIComponent)
   - Important: `last:border-b-0` overridden by inline style (fixed by switching to Tailwind classes)
   - Nitpick: `&` in JSX (fixed)
   - Committed as `bf19dce`

8. **Visual verification**: Dev server started, navigated to the actual semantic release page. The snapshot shows:
   - Version heading "v1.16.7"
   - Subject line rendered
   - HIGH RISK banner with urgency and risk_reason
   - Status checks (Docker Image Verified, Binaries Available)
   - Download links (github.com, geth.ethereum.org, hub.docker.com)
   - Download commands (docker pull commands with copy buttons)
   - Adoption section
   - Changelog Summary
   - Recommendation blockquote
   - NOTE: Source releases section is missing (404 error on `/sources` endpoint - the backend likely needs to be restarted to pick up the new route)
   - NOTE: Context sources section not showing (may not have any configured)

9. **User's latest message**: "Can you have a download link button directly into the binary for different platforms, current it points to a general release page" â€” This is a NEW request. The user wants platform-specific binary download buttons instead of generic links to the release page. Currently the download_links array contains URLs like `https://github.com/ethereum/go-ethereum/releases/tag/v1.16.7` which goes to the general release page. The user wants direct links to platform-specific binaries (e.g., Linux AMD64, macOS ARM64, Windows, etc.).

Then the user asked for a conversation summary, so I should NOT implement the new request yet but include it as a pending task.

Key files:
- `internal/api/semantic_releases.go` - Added ListSources handler
- `internal/api/server.go` - Registered new route
- `internal/api/semantic_releases_test.go` - Added tests
- `web/lib/api/types.ts` - Updated SemanticReport interface
- `web/lib/api/client.ts` - Added getSources method  
- `web/components/semantic-releases/semantic-release-detail.tsx` - Full rewrite

Errors encountered:
- 404 on `/semantic-releases/{id}/sources` endpoint during visual verification - backend needs restart
- Code review found 4 issues that were fixed before committing

All user messages:
1. Initial request to redesign with full example data
2. Answered "Yes, add new endpoint (Recommended)" for source releases API
3. "Looks good" - approved the design
4. "1" - chose subagent-driven execution
5. "Can you have a download link button directly into the binary for different platforms, current it points to a general release page" - NEW feature request

Pending tasks:
- Task 5 (Visual verification) - in progress, screenshot taken but errors noted
- Task 6 (Run all tests) - pending
- NEW: User wants platform-specific binary download buttons instead of generic release page links

Summary:
1. Primary Request and Intent:
   The user requested a redesign of the semantic release detail page (`/projects/{projectId}/semantic-releases/{srId}`). The current page was missing critical fields from the backend data model. Using go-ethereum v1.16.7 as an example, the user showed the full data shape including `subject`, `risk_level`, `risk_reason`, `status_checks`, `download_links`, `download_commands`, `changelog_summary`, etc. The user specified the desired layout should highlight: docker image/binary availability with download links, risk level, urgency, adoption (optional), related source versions (clickable â€” currently showing ALL project versions instead of only associated ones), context source links, and a changelog summary.

   **Most recent user request (not yet implemented):** "Can you have a download link button directly into the binary for different platforms, current it points to a general release page" â€” The user wants platform-specific binary download buttons (e.g., Linux AMD64, macOS ARM64, Windows) instead of generic links to the GitHub release page.

2. Key Technical Concepts:
   - Go backend (net/http) with `SemanticReleasesHandler` and `SemanticReleasesStore` interface
   - Next.js 15+ frontend with React, Tailwind CSS, SWR for data fetching
   - Font system: Fraunces (display headings), DM Sans (body), JetBrains Mono (code/versions)
   - Color palette: `#e8601a` accent orange, `#111113` primary text, `#6b7280` secondary, `#9ca3af` muted
   - Custom UI components: `StatusDot`, `VersionChip`, `SectionLabel`, `ProviderBadge`, `UrgencyCallout` (now replaced)
   - `semantic_release_sources` join table linking semantic releases to specific source releases
   - Backend stores `report` as `json.RawMessage` â€” the frontend receives the full JSON object

3. Files and Code Sections:

   - **`internal/api/semantic_releases.go`**
     - Added `ListSources` handler method to expose linked source releases via HTTP
     ```go
     func (h *SemanticReleasesHandler) ListSources(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {
         id := r.PathValue("id")
         if id == "" {
             RespondError(w, r, http.StatusBadRequest, "bad_request", "Semantic release ID is required")
             return
         }
         releases, err := h.store.GetSemanticReleaseSources(r.Context(), id)
         if err != nil {
             RespondError(w, r, http.StatusNotFound, "not_found", "Semantic release not found")
             return
         }
         if releases == nil {
             releases = []models.Release{}
         }
         RespondJSON(w, r, http.StatusOK, releases)
     }
     ```

   - **`internal/api/server.go`**
     - Registered new route after existing semantic-releases DELETE route:
     ```go
     mux.Handle("GET /api/v1/semantic-releases/{id}/sources", chain(http.HandlerFunc(semanticReleases.ListSources)))
     ```

   - **`internal/api/semantic_releases_test.go`**
     - Updated `setupSemanticReleasesMux` to include the new route
     - Added `TestSemanticReleasesHandlerListSources` and `TestSemanticReleasesHandlerListSourcesEmpty` tests
     - Mock store already had `GetSemanticReleaseSources` method and `sources`/`sourcesErr` fields

   - **`web/lib/api/types.ts`** (lines 106-119)
     - Updated `SemanticReport` interface from 5 required fields to 12 optional fields:
     ```typescript
     export interface SemanticReport {
       subject?: string;
       risk_level?: string;
       risk_reason?: string;
       status_checks?: string[];
       changelog_summary?: string;
       download_commands?: string[];
       download_links?: string[];
       summary?: string;
       availability?: string;
       adoption?: string;
       urgency?: string;
       recommendation?: string;
     }
     ```

   - **`web/lib/api/client.ts`** (lines 115-128)
     - Added `getSources` method to `semanticReleases` object:
     ```typescript
     getSources: (id: string) =>
       request<ApiResponse<Release[]>>(`/semantic-releases/${id}/sources`),
     ```

   - **`web/components/semantic-releases/semantic-release-detail.tsx`** (FULL REWRITE)
     - This is the main component. Complete rewrite with new sections:
     - **Data fetching changes**: Removed `releasesApi.listByProject(projectId)`, added `srApi.getSources(srId)` and `contextSources.list(projectId)`
     - **Removed**: `UrgencyCallout` import
     - **Added imports**: `contextSources` from API client, `Check`, `ExternalLink`, `Copy`, `ShieldAlert`, `BookOpen` from lucide-react
     - **New helper functions**: `getRiskColors(riskLevel)` maps CRITICAL/HIGH/MEDIUM/LOW to colors, `safeHostname(url)` extracts domain with try-catch
     - **New sections in order**: Subject line (20px), Risk & Urgency Banner (color-coded card with risk badge + urgency + risk_reason), Status Checks & Downloads (green pills + clickable link pills + copiable code blocks), Adoption (conditional card), Source Releases (from getSources with clickable version links), Context Sources (from contextSources API), Changelog Summary (prose), Recommendation (blockquote with orange border)
     - **Version links**: GitHub â†’ `https://github.com/{repo}/releases/tag/{version}`, Docker Hub â†’ `https://hub.docker.com/r/{repo}/tags?name=${encodeURIComponent(version)}`

   - **`internal/models/semantic_release.go`** (read only)
     - Go backend model showing all available fields in `SemanticReport` struct

   - **`docs/plans/2026-02-28-semantic-release-detail-redesign.md`**
     - Design doc and implementation plan with 6 tasks

4. Errors and Fixes:
   - **Code review issue: Delete has no error handling** â€” `handleDelete` called `await srApi.delete(srId)` without try/catch. Fixed by wrapping in try/catch with `alert("Failed to delete semantic release.")` fallback.
   - **Code review issue: Docker Hub URL not encoded** â€” `tags?name=${rel.version}` could break with special characters. Fixed with `encodeURIComponent(rel.version)`.
   - **Code review issue: `last:border-b-0` not working** â€” Inline `style={{ borderBottom: "1px solid #e8e8e5" }}` overrides Tailwind's `last:border-b-0` due to CSS specificity. Fixed by switching to `className="border-b border-[#e8e8e5] last:border-b-0"`.
   - **Code review issue: `&` in JSX** â€” Unnecessary HTML entity. Fixed to plain `&`.
   - **Visual verification: 404 on `/semantic-releases/{id}/sources`** â€” The backend was not restarted after adding the new endpoint. The error shows in the browser console during visual verification. Not yet resolved.

5. Problem Solving:
   - Solved the data gap between Go backend (12 fields) and frontend TypeScript types (5 fields) by updating the interface
   - Solved the "shows ALL project releases" problem by adding a new API endpoint that queries the `semantic_release_sources` join table via the existing `GetSemanticReleaseSources` store method
   - Replaced the limited `UrgencyCallout` component with an inline Risk & Urgency Banner that shows risk_level, urgency, and risk_reason together
   - Added download commands with clipboard copy functionality
   - Made version chips clickable with provider-specific URLs

6. All User Messages:
   - **Message 1**: "Redesign the semantic release detail page, for example the eth v1.16.7's semantic release is {full JSON data}... however page http://localhost:3000/projects/f7faca53-148e-4e87-b19c-c31ddeca389e/semantic-releases/57084bf4-6cb7-4d49-b99a-25e429995b73 doesn't contain all the information like download link and we should highlight the important information like docker image/binary available with download link, risk level, urgency, adoption(optional) then related sources version(clickable, currently all the versions of the sources are associated), and context sources links (if applicable), following with a summary of the release"
   - **Message 2**: Answered clarifying question: "Yes, add new endpoint (Recommended)" for source releases API
   - **Message 3**: "Looks good" â€” approved the design proposal
   - **Message 4**: "1" â€” chose subagent-driven execution (this session)
   - **Message 5**: "Can you have a download link button directly into the binary for different platforms, current it points to a general release page" â€” NEW unimplemented request

7. Pending Tasks:
   - **Task 5 (Visual verification)**: In progress â€” screenshot taken, page renders correctly but the `/sources` endpoint returns 404 (backend needs restart to pick up the new route)
   - **Task 6 (Run all tests)**: Pending â€” `go test ./internal/api/...` and `cd web && npm run build`
   - **NEW REQUEST**: User wants platform-specific binary download buttons instead of generic release page links. Currently `download_links` contains URLs like `https://github.com/ethereum/go-ethereum/releases/tag/v1.16.7` which links to the general release page. The user wants direct download buttons for specific platform binaries (e.g., geth-linux-amd64, geth-darwin-arm64, etc.)

8. Current Work:
   The implementation of the semantic release detail page redesign is nearly complete (Tasks 1-4 done, Task 5 in progress, Task 6 pending). The page was visually verified via Playwright â€” all new sections render correctly (subject, risk banner, status checks, download links, download commands, adoption, changelog summary, recommendation). Two issues remain: (1) the `/semantic-releases/{id}/sources` endpoint returns 404 because the backend hasn't been restarted, and (2) the source releases and context sources sections may not appear until the backend serves the new endpoint.

   The user's most recent message introduces a **new feature request** about platform-specific binary downloads that was NOT part of the original redesign plan.

9. Optional Next Step:
   The user's most recent request was: **"Can you have a download link button directly into the binary for different platforms, current it points to a general release page"**. This means the download links section currently shows generic release page URLs (e.g., `github.com`, `geth.ethereum.org`) but the user wants direct platform-specific binary download buttons. This would likely involve either:
   - Parsing the GitHub release API to extract platform-specific asset URLs (`.tar.gz`, `.zip` files for linux-amd64, darwin-arm64, windows-amd64, etc.)
   - Or adding a new field to the semantic report for platform-specific binaries
   - Or scraping/resolving the download links at display time

   Before implementing, I should clarify the approach with the user since the `download_links` data comes from the AI agent's report output and may need backend/prompt changes to include platform-specific URLs.

1â†’package agent
     2â†’
     3â†’import (
     4â†’	"context"
     5â†’	"fmt"
     6â†’
     7â†’	"google.golang.org/adk/model"
     8â†’	"google.golang.org/adk/model/gemini"
     9â†’	"google.golang.org/genai"
    10â†’
    11â†’	oaimodel "github.com/sentioxyz/changelogue/internal/agent/openai"
    12â†’)
    13â†’
    14â†’// LLMConfig holds the configuration for creating an LLM model.
    15â†’type LLMConfig struct {
    16â†’	Provider string // "gemini" or "openai"
    17â†’	Model    string // e.g. "gemini-2.5-flash", "gpt-5.2"
    18â†’
    19â†’	// Gemini
    20â†’	GoogleAPIKey string
    21â†’
    22â†’	// OpenAI
    23â†’	OpenAIAPIKey  string
    24â†’	OpenAIBaseURL string // defaults to https://api.openai.com/v1
    25â†’}
    26â†’
    27â†’// NewLLMModel creates a model.LLM based on the configured provider.
    28â†’func NewLLMModel(ctx context.Context, cfg LLMConfig) (model.LLM, error) {
    29â†’	switch cfg.Provider {
    30â†’	case "gemini", "":
    31â†’		if cfg.GoogleAPIKey == "" {
    32â†’			return nil, fmt.Errorf("GOOGLE_API_KEY is required for gemini provider")
    33â†’		}
    34â†’		return gemini.NewModel(ctx, cfg.Model, &genai.ClientConfig{
    35â†’			APIKey: cfg.GoogleAPIKey,
    36â†’		})
    37â†’
    38â†’	case "openai":
    39â†’		return oaimodel.NewModel(ctx, cfg.Model, oaimodel.Config{
    40â†’			APIKey:  cfg.OpenAIAPIKey,
    41â†’			BaseURL: cfg.OpenAIBaseURL,
    42â†’		})
    43â†’
    44â†’	default:
    45â†’		return nil, fmt.Errorf("unknown LLM provider: %q (supported: gemini, openai)", cfg.Provider)
    46â†’	}
    47â†’}
    48â†’

1â†’package agent
     2â†’
     3â†’import (
     4â†’	"context"
     5â†’	"encoding/json"
     6â†’	"fmt"
     7â†’	"log/slog"
     8â†’	"net/http"
     9â†’	"strings"
    10â†’	"time"
    11â†’
    12â†’	"google.golang.org/genai"
    13â†’
    14â†’	"google.golang.org/adk/agent"
    15â†’	"google.golang.org/adk/agent/llmagent"
    16â†’	"google.golang.org/adk/plugin"
    17â†’	"google.golang.org/adk/plugin/loggingplugin"
    18â†’	"google.golang.org/adk/runner"
    19â†’	"google.golang.org/adk/session"
    20â†’	"google.golang.org/adk/tool"
    21â†’	"google.golang.org/adk/tool/agenttool"
    22â†’	"google.golang.org/adk/tool/geminitool"
    23â†’
    24â†’	"github.com/sentioxyz/changelogue/internal/models"
    25â†’	"github.com/sentioxyz/changelogue/internal/routing"
    26â†’)
    27â†’
    28â†’// OrchestratorStore defines all data access methods required by the agent
    29â†’// orchestrator to load project configuration, run the agent, and persist
    30â†’// the resulting semantic release.
    31â†’type OrchestratorStore interface {
    32â†’	AgentDataStore
    33â†’	GetProject(ctx context.Context, id string) (*models.Project, error)
    34â†’	GetAgentRun(ctx context.Context, id string) (*models.AgentRun, error)
    35â†’	UpdateAgentRunStatus(ctx context.Context, id, status string) error
    36â†’	CreateSemanticRelease(ctx context.Context, sr *models.SemanticRelease, releaseIDs []string) error
    37â†’	UpdateAgentRunResult(ctx context.Context, id string, semanticReleaseID string) error
    38â†’	ListProjectSubscriptions(ctx context.Context, projectID string) ([]models.Subscription, error)
    39â†’	GetChannel(ctx context.Context, id string) (*models.NotificationChannel, error)
    40â†’	ListSourcesByProject(ctx context.Context, projectID string, page, perPage int) ([]models.Source, int, error)
    41â†’	HasReleaseForVersion(ctx context.Context, sourceID, version string) (bool, error)
    42â†’}
    43â†’
    44â†’const DefaultInstruction = `You are a release intelligence agent analyzing version {{VERSION}} of a software project.
    45â†’
    46â†’Focus ONLY on version {{VERSION}}. Cross-check this version across all available sources.
    47â†’
    48â†’Use the available tools to:
    49â†’1. Fetch releases and find the one matching {{VERSION}} from each source.
    50â†’2. Inspect release details (changelogs, commit data, raw payloads) for {{VERSION}} only.
    51â†’3. Check binary/image availability directly from the source data.
    52â†’4. Review the project's context sources (runbooks, documentation) for relevant background.
    53â†’5. Use web search ONLY when you need additional context not available from sources
    54â†’   (e.g., community sentiment, security advisories, network adoption stats, known issues).
    55â†’
    56â†’CRITICAL: Your final response MUST be a single JSON object and nothing else.
    57â†’Do not include any explanation, commentary, or markdown formatting â€” just the raw JSON.
    58â†’
    59â†’The JSON object must have exactly these fields:
    60â†’{
    61â†’  "subject": "Ready to Deploy: <Project> <Version> (<Risk Summary>)",
    62â†’  "risk_level": "CRITICAL|HIGH|MEDIUM|LOW",
    63â†’  "risk_reason": "Why this risk level (e.g., 'Hard Fork detected in Discord #announcements')",
    64â†’  "status_checks": ["Docker Image Verified", "Binaries Available"],
    65â†’  "changelog_summary": "One-line summary of key changes (e.g., 'Fixes sync bug in block 14,000,000')",
    66â†’  "availability": "GA|RC|Beta",
    67â†’  "adoption": "Percentage or recommendation (e.g., '12% of network updated (Wait recommended if not urgent)')",
    68â†’  "urgency": "Critical|High|Medium|Low",
    69â†’  "recommendation": "Actionable 1-2 sentence recommendation for the SRE team",
    70â†’  "download_commands": ["docker pull ethereum/client-go:v1.10.15"],
    71â†’  "download_links": ["https://github.com/ethereum/go-ethereum/releases/tag/v1.10.15"]
    72â†’}`
    73â†’
    74â†’// BuildAgent creates the ADK-Go LLM agent for a given project. This is used
    75â†’// by both the production orchestrator and the dev entrypoint to ensure the
    76â†’// same agent configuration is used everywhere.
    77â†’func BuildAgent(ctx context.Context, store AgentDataStore, project *models.Project, llmConfig LLMConfig, version string) (agent.Agent, error) {
    78â†’	instruction := DefaultInstruction
    79â†’	if project.AgentPrompt != "" {
    80â†’		instruction = project.AgentPrompt + "\n\n" + instruction
    81â†’	}
    82â†’	// Substitute version placeholder
    83â†’	instruction = strings.ReplaceAll(instruction, "{{VERSION}}", version)
    84â†’
    85â†’	llmModel, err := NewLLMModel(ctx, llmConfig)
    86â†’	if err != nil {
    87â†’		return nil, fmt.Errorf("create LLM model: %w", err)
    88â†’	}
    89â†’
    90â†’	// Create project-scoped function tools.
    91â†’	functionTools, err := NewTools(store, project.ID)
    92â†’	if err != nil {
    93â†’		return nil, fmt.Errorf("create agent tools: %w", err)
    94â†’	}
    95â†’
    96â†’	// For Gemini: use sub-agent pattern (required because Gemini can't mix
    97â†’	// grounding tools like GoogleSearch with function tools on the same agent).
    98â†’	// For OpenAI: flat architecture â€” give function tools directly to root agent
    99â†’	// (OpenAI doesn't support the agenttool schema).
   100â†’	if llmConfig.Provider == "openai" {
   101â†’		return llmagent.New(llmagent.Config{
   102â†’			Name:        "release_analyst",
   103â†’			Description: "Analyzes upstream releases and produces semantic release reports.",
   104â†’			Model:       llmModel,
   105â†’			Instruction: instruction,
   106â†’			Tools:       functionTools,
   107â†’		})
   108â†’	}
   109â†’
   110â†’	// Gemini path: sub-agent architecture.
   111â†’
   112â†’	// Data sub-agent: handles DB queries for releases and context sources.
   113â†’	dataAgent, err := llmagent.New(llmagent.Config{
   114â†’		Name:        "data_agent",
   115â†’		Description: "Query project releases and context sources from the database. Use this to fetch release lists, release details, and context sources like runbooks and documentation.",
   116â†’		Model:       llmModel,
   117â†’		Tools:       functionTools,
   118â†’	})
   119â†’	if err != nil {
   120â†’		return nil, fmt.Errorf("create data sub-agent: %w", err)
   121â†’	}
   122â†’
   123â†’	// Search sub-agent: Google Search grounding.
   124â†’	searchAgent, err := llmagent.New(llmagent.Config{
   125â†’		Name:        "search_agent",
   126â†’		Description: "Search the web for additional context about a release. Use this ONLY when you need information not available from the project's sources, such as community sentiment, security advisories, network adoption statistics, or known issues.",
   127â†’		Model:       llmModel,
   128â†’		Tools:       []tool.Tool{geminitool.GoogleSearch{}},
   129â†’	})
   130â†’	if err != nil {
   131â†’		return nil, fmt.Errorf("create search sub-agent: %w", err)
   132â†’	}
   133â†’
   134â†’	// Root agent orchestrates data lookup and web search via sub-agents.
   135â†’	return llmagent.New(llmagent.Config{
   136â†’		Name:        "release_analyst",
   137â†’		Description: "Analyzes upstream releases and produces semantic release reports.",
   138â†’		Model:       llmModel,
   139â†’		Instruction: instruction,
   140â†’		Tools: []tool.Tool{
   141â†’			agenttool.New(dataAgent, nil),
   142â†’			agenttool.New(searchAgent, nil),
   143â†’		},
   144â†’	})
   145â†’}
   146â†’
   147â†’// checkAllSourcesReady returns true if every source in the project has a
   148â†’// release matching the target version.
   149â†’func (o *Orchestrator) checkAllSourcesReady(ctx context.Context, projectID, version string) (bool, error) {
   150â†’	// Use a high limit to fetch all sources; projects rarely exceed a handful.
   151â†’	sources, _, err := o.store.ListSourcesByProject(ctx, projectID, 1, 1000)
   152â†’	if err != nil {
   153â†’		return false, fmt.Errorf("list sources: %w", err)
   154â†’	}
   155â†’	if len(sources) == 0 {
   156â†’		return true, nil
   157â†’	}
   158â†’
   159â†’	for _, src := range sources {
   160â†’		has, err := o.store.HasReleaseForVersion(ctx, src.ID, version)
   161â†’		if err != nil {
   162â†’			return false, fmt.Errorf("check source %s: %w", src.ID, err)
   163â†’		}
   164â†’		if !has {
   165â†’			slog.Info("agent: source not ready for version",
   166â†’				"project_id", projectID,
   167â†’				"source_id", src.ID,
   168â†’				"version", version,
   169â†’			)
   170â†’			return false, nil
   171â†’		}
   172â†’	}
   173â†’	return true, nil
   174â†’}
   175â†’
   176â†’// Orchestrator manages the lifecycle of an agent run: loading project config,
   177â†’// creating an ADK-Go agent with project-specific tools and instructions,
   178â†’// running the agent, and persisting the result as a semantic release.
   179â†’type Orchestrator struct {
   180â†’	store     OrchestratorStore
   181â†’	llmConfig LLMConfig
   182â†’}
   183â†’
   184â†’// NewOrchestrator creates a new Orchestrator. It validates that the provided
   185â†’// LLMConfig has the required API key for the configured provider. If the key
   186â†’// is missing, it returns an error so the caller can degrade gracefully.
   187â†’func NewOrchestrator(store OrchestratorStore, cfg LLMConfig) (*Orchestrator, error) {
   188â†’	switch cfg.Provider {
   189â†’	case "openai":
   190â†’		if cfg.OpenAIAPIKey == "" {
   191â†’			return nil, fmt.Errorf("OPENAI_API_KEY is not set; agent orchestrator requires an LLM API key")
   192â†’		}
   193â†’	default: // gemini
   194â†’		if cfg.GoogleAPIKey == "" {
   195â†’			return nil, fmt.Errorf("GOOGLE_API_KEY is not set; agent orchestrator requires an LLM API key")
   196â†’		}
   197â†’	}
   198â†’	return &Orchestrator{
   199â†’		store:     store,
   200â†’		llmConfig: cfg,
   201â†’	}, nil
   202â†’}
   203â†’
   204â†’// RunAgent executes the full agent lifecycle for a given agent run:
   205â†’//  1. Marks the run as "running".
   206â†’//  2. Loads the project and builds the agent prompt.
   207â†’//  3. Creates an ADK-Go LLM agent with project-scoped tools.
   208â†’//  4. Runs the agent and captures its final text response.
   209â†’//  5. Parses the response into a SemanticReport.
   210â†’//  6. Creates a SemanticRelease and links it to the agent run.
   211â†’//  7. Marks the run as "completed" (or "failed" on error).
   212â†’func (o *Orchestrator) RunAgent(ctx context.Context, run *models.AgentRun) error {
   213â†’	// Mark as running.
   214â†’	if err := o.store.UpdateAgentRunStatus(ctx, run.ID, "running"); err != nil {
   215â†’		return fmt.Errorf("update agent run status to running: %w", err)
   216â†’	}
   217â†’
   218â†’	// Execute the agent; capture any error to mark the run as failed.
   219â†’	result, err := o.executeAgent(ctx, run)
   220â†’	if err != nil {
   221â†’		slog.Error("agent run failed", "run_id", run.ID, "err", err)
   222â†’		// Best-effort: mark the run as failed.
   223â†’		if statusErr := o.store.UpdateAgentRunStatus(ctx, run.ID, "failed"); statusErr != nil {
   224â†’			slog.Error("agent: failed to mark run as failed",
   225â†’				"run_id", run.ID, "status_err", statusErr)
   226â†’		}
   227â†’		return err
   228â†’	}
   229â†’
   230â†’	// Link the semantic release to the agent run and mark completed.
   231â†’	if err := o.store.UpdateAgentRunResult(ctx, run.ID, result.semanticReleaseID); err != nil {
   232â†’		return fmt.Errorf("update agent run result: %w", err)
   233â†’	}
   234â†’	if err := o.store.UpdateAgentRunStatus(ctx, run.ID, "completed"); err != nil {
   235â†’		return fmt.Errorf("update agent run status to completed: %w", err)
   236â†’	}
   237â†’
   238â†’	// Send project-level notifications (best-effort; errors are logged, not returned).
   239â†’	o.sendProjectNotifications(ctx, run, result)
   240â†’
   241â†’	return nil
   242â†’}
   243â†’
   244â†’// agentResult holds the output of a successful agent execution, used to
   245â†’// construct notifications after the semantic release is persisted.
   246â†’type agentResult struct {
   247â†’	semanticReleaseID string
   248â†’	version           string
   249â†’	reportText        string
   250â†’	projectName       string
   251â†’}
   252â†’
   253â†’// executeAgent performs the actual LLM agent interaction and returns the
   254â†’// agentResult containing the semantic release ID and metadata for notifications.
   255â†’func (o *Orchestrator) executeAgent(ctx context.Context, run *models.AgentRun) (*agentResult, error) {
   256â†’	// Load project.
   257â†’	slog.Info("agent: loading project", "run_id", run.ID, "project_id", run.ProjectID)
   258â†’	project, err := o.store.GetProject(ctx, run.ProjectID)
   259â†’	if err != nil {
   260â†’		return nil, fmt.Errorf("get project: %w", err)
   261â†’	}
   262â†’	slog.Info("agent: project loaded", "run_id", run.ID, "project", project.Name)
   263â†’
   264â†’	// Extract target version from the agent run.
   265â†’	version := run.Version
   266â†’	if version == "" {
   267â†’		// Fallback: parse from trigger "auto:version:v1.10.15"
   268â†’		if strings.HasPrefix(run.Trigger, "auto:version:") {
   269â†’			version = strings.TrimPrefix(run.Trigger, "auto:version:")
   270â†’		}
   271â†’	}
   272â†’
   273â†’	// Build the agent using the shared constructor.
   274â†’	slog.Info("agent: building agent", "run_id", run.ID,
   275â†’		"provider", o.llmConfig.Provider, "model", o.llmConfig.Model,
   276â†’		"version", version)
   277â†’	agentInstance, err := BuildAgent(ctx, o.store, project, o.llmConfig, version)
   278â†’	if err != nil {
   279â†’		return nil, fmt.Errorf("build agent: %w", err)
   280â†’	}
   281â†’
   282â†’	// Create in-memory session service and a new session.
   283â†’	sessionService := session.InMemoryService()
   284â†’	createResp, err := sessionService.Create(ctx, &session.CreateRequest{
   285â†’		AppName: "changelogue",
   286â†’		UserID:  "system",
   287â†’	})
   288â†’	if err != nil {
   289â†’		return nil, fmt.Errorf("create session: %w", err)
   290â†’	}
   291â†’	sess := createResp.Session
   292â†’
   293â†’	// Create the runner with logging plugin for agent trace visibility.
   294â†’	r, err := runner.New(runner.Config{
   295â†’		AppName:        "changelogue",
   296â†’		Agent:          agentInstance,
   297â†’		SessionService: sessionService,
   298â†’		PluginConfig: runner.PluginConfig{
   299â†’			Plugins:      []*plugin.Plugin{loggingplugin.MustNew("agent_trace")},
   300â†’			CloseTimeout: 5 * time.Second,
   301â†’		},
   302â†’	})
   303â†’	if err != nil {
   304â†’		return nil, fmt.Errorf("create runner: %w", err)
   305â†’	}
   306â†’
   307â†’	// Run the agent with a prompt requesting analysis.
   308â†’	slog.Info("agent: starting LLM run", "run_id", run.ID, "project", project.Name, "version", version)
   309â†’	var userPrompt string
   310â†’	if version != "" {
   311â†’		userPrompt = fmt.Sprintf("Analyze version %s for this project. Cross-check all sources and produce a semantic release report.", version)
   312â†’	} else {
   313â†’		userPrompt = "Analyze the recent releases for this project and produce a semantic release report."
   314â†’	}
   315â†’	userMsg := genai.NewContentFromText(userPrompt, "user")
   316â†’
   317â†’	var finalText string
   318â†’	eventCount := 0
   319â†’	for event, err := range r.Run(ctx, "system", sess.ID(), userMsg, agent.RunConfig{}) {
   320â†’		if err != nil {
   321â†’			slog.Error("agent: run event error",
   322â†’				"run_id", run.ID,
   323â†’				"event_count", eventCount,
   324â†’				"err", err,
   325â†’			)
   326â†’			return nil, fmt.Errorf("agent run event error: %w", err)
   327â†’		}
   328â†’		eventCount++
   329â†’		if event != nil && event.IsFinalResponse() && event.Content != nil {
   330â†’			for _, part := range event.Content.Parts {
   331â†’				if part.Text != "" {
   332â†’					finalText += part.Text
   333â†’				}
   334â†’			}
   335â†’		}
   336â†’	}
   337â†’	slog.Info("agent: LLM run finished",
   338â†’		"run_id", run.ID,
   339â†’		"event_count", eventCount,
   340â†’		"output_length", len(finalText),
   341â†’	)
   342â†’
   343â†’	if finalText == "" {
   344â†’		slog.Error("agent: produced no output",
   345â†’			"run_id", run.ID,
   346â†’			"project_id", run.ProjectID,
   347â†’			"event_count", eventCount,
   348â†’		)
   349â†’		return nil, fmt.Errorf("agent produced no output")
   350â†’	}
   351â†’
   352â†’	// Parse the agent's response into a SemanticReport.
   353â†’	report, err := parseReport(finalText)
   354â†’	if err != nil {
   355â†’		slog.Warn("agent output was not valid JSON report, storing raw",
   356â†’			"run_id", run.ID, "parse_err", err, "raw_output", finalText)
   357â†’		// Fall back to storing the raw text as the summary.
   358â†’		report = &models.SemanticReport{
   359â†’			Summary: finalText,
   360â†’		}
   361â†’	}
   362â†’
   363â†’	reportJSON, err := json.Marshal(report)
   364â†’	if err != nil {
   365â†’		return nil, fmt.Errorf("marshal report: %w", err)
   366â†’	}
   367â†’
   368â†’	// Gather release IDs for the semantic_release_sources join table.
   369â†’	releases, _, err := o.store.ListReleasesByProject(ctx, run.ProjectID, 1, 50)
   370â†’	if err != nil {
   371â†’		return nil, fmt.Errorf("list releases for semantic release: %w", err)
   372â†’	}
   373â†’	releaseIDs := make([]string, 0, len(releases))
   374â†’	for _, r := range releases {
   375â†’		releaseIDs = append(releaseIDs, r.ID)
   376â†’	}
   377â†’
   378â†’	// Use the target version for the semantic release.
   379â†’	srVersion := version
   380â†’	if srVersion == "" {
   381â†’		srVersion = "unknown"
   382â†’		if len(releases) > 0 {
   383â†’			srVersion = releases[0].Version
   384â†’		}
   385â†’	}
   386â†’
   387â†’	now := time.Now()
   388â†’	sr := &models.SemanticRelease{
   389â†’		ProjectID:   run.ProjectID,
   390â†’		Version:     srVersion,
   391â†’		Report:      reportJSON,
   392â†’		Status:      "completed",
   393â†’		CompletedAt: &now,
   394â†’	}
   395â†’
   396â†’	if err := o.store.CreateSemanticRelease(ctx, sr, releaseIDs); err != nil {
   397â†’		return nil, fmt.Errorf("create semantic release: %w", err)
   398â†’	}
   399â†’
   400â†’	slog.Info("agent run produced semantic release",
   401â†’		"run_id", run.ID,
   402â†’		"semantic_release_id", sr.ID,
   403â†’		"version", sr.Version,
   404â†’	)
   405â†’
   406â†’	return &agentResult{
   407â†’		semanticReleaseID: sr.ID,
   408â†’		version:           srVersion,
   409â†’		reportText:        finalText,
   410â†’		projectName:       project.Name,
   411â†’	}, nil
   412â†’}
   413â†’
   414â†’// defaultSenders returns a map of channel type to Sender for the supported
   415â†’// notification channel types (webhook, slack, discord).
   416â†’func defaultSenders() map[string]routing.Sender {
   417â†’	httpClient := &http.Client{Timeout: 10 * time.Second}
   418â†’	return map[string]routing.Sender{
   419â†’		"webhook": &routing.WebhookSender{Client: httpClient},
   420â†’		"slack":   &routing.SlackSender{Client: httpClient},
   421â†’		"discord": &routing.DiscordSender{Client: httpClient},
   422â†’	}
   423â†’}
   424â†’
   425â†’// sendProjectNotifications looks up project-level subscriptions and sends a
   426â†’// notification to each channel with the semantic release report. This is
   427â†’// best-effort: errors are logged but do not propagate to the caller.
   428â†’func (o *Orchestrator) sendProjectNotifications(ctx context.Context, run *models.AgentRun, result *agentResult) {
   429â†’	subs, err := o.store.ListProjectSubscriptions(ctx, run.ProjectID)
   430â†’	if err != nil {
   431â†’		slog.Error("list project subscriptions for notification",
   432â†’			"project_id", run.ProjectID, "err", err)
   433â†’		return
   434â†’	}
   435â†’	if len(subs) == 0 {
   436â†’		slog.Debug("agent: no project subscriptions configured",
   437â†’			"project_id", run.ProjectID)
   438â†’		return
   439â†’	}
   440â†’
   441â†’	senders := defaultSenders()
   442â†’
   443â†’	msg := routing.Notification{
   444â†’		Title:   fmt.Sprintf("Semantic Release Report: %s %s", result.projectName, result.version),
   445â†’		Body:    result.reportText,
   446â†’		Version: result.version,
   447â†’	}
   448â†’
   449â†’	for _, sub := range subs {
   450â†’		ch, err := o.store.GetChannel(ctx, sub.ChannelID)
   451â†’		if err != nil {
   452â†’			slog.Error("get channel for project notification",
   453â†’				"channel_id", sub.ChannelID, "err", err)
   454â†’			continue
   455â†’		}
   456â†’
   457â†’		sender, ok := senders[ch.Type]
   458â†’		if !ok {
   459â†’			slog.Warn("unknown channel type for project notification", "type", ch.Type)
   460â†’			continue
   461â†’		}
   462â†’
   463â†’		if err := sender.Send(ctx, ch, msg); err != nil {
   464â†’			slog.Error("send project notification failed",
   465â†’				"channel", ch.Name, "type", ch.Type, "err", err)
   466â†’		} else {
   467â†’			slog.Info("project notification sent",
   468â†’				"channel", ch.Name, "project_id", run.ProjectID, "version", result.version)
   469â†’		}
   470â†’	}
   471â†’}
   472â†’
   473â†’// parseReport attempts to parse the agent's text output as a SemanticReport JSON.
   474â†’// It handles cases where the agent wraps the JSON in markdown code blocks.
   475â†’func parseReport(text string) (*models.SemanticReport, error) {
   476â†’	// Strip markdown code fences if present.
   477â†’	cleaned := strings.TrimSpace(text)
   478â†’	if strings.HasPrefix(cleaned, "```") {
   479â†’		// Remove opening fence (with optional language tag).
   480â†’		if idx := strings.Index(cleaned, "\n"); idx != -1 {
   481â†’			cleaned = cleaned[idx+1:]
   482â†’		}
   483â†’		// Remove closing fence.
   484â†’		if idx := strings.LastIndex(cleaned, "```"); idx != -1 {
   485â†’			cleaned = cleaned[:idx]
   486â†’		}
   487â†’		cleaned = strings.TrimSpace(cleaned)
   488â†’	}
   489â†’
   490â†’	var report models.SemanticReport
   491â†’	if err := json.Unmarshal([]byte(cleaned), &report); err != nil {
   492â†’		return nil, fmt.Errorf("parse report JSON: %w", err)
   493â†’	}
   494â†’
   495â†’	if report.Subject == "" && report.Summary == "" {
   496â†’		return nil, fmt.Errorf("report is missing required 'subject' or 'summary' field")
   497â†’	}
   498â†’
   499â†’	return &report, nil
   500â†’}
   501â†’

1â†’package agent
     2â†’
     3â†’import (
     4â†’	"context"
     5â†’	"fmt"
     6â†’
     7â†’	"google.golang.org/adk/tool"
     8â†’	"google.golang.org/adk/tool/functiontool"
     9â†’
    10â†’	"github.com/sentioxyz/changelogue/internal/models"
    11â†’)
    12â†’
    13â†’// AgentDataStore defines the data access methods that agent tools use to
    14â†’// research releases and context sources for a given project.
    15â†’type AgentDataStore interface {
    16â†’	ListReleasesByProject(ctx context.Context, projectID string, page, perPage int) ([]models.Release, int, error)
    17â†’	GetRelease(ctx context.Context, id string) (*models.Release, error)
    18â†’	ListContextSources(ctx context.Context, projectID string, page, perPage int) ([]models.ContextSource, int, error)
    19â†’}
    20â†’
    21â†’// --- Tool input/output types ---
    22â†’
    23â†’// GetReleasesInput is the input for the get_releases tool.
    24â†’type GetReleasesInput struct {
    25â†’	Page    int `json:"page,omitempty"`
    26â†’	PerPage int `json:"per_page,omitempty"`
    27â†’}
    28â†’
    29â†’// ReleaseItem is a single release in the tool output.
    30â†’type ReleaseItem struct {
    31â†’	ID         string `json:"id"`
    32â†’	SourceID   string `json:"source_id"`
    33â†’	Version    string `json:"version"`
    34â†’	RawData    any    `json:"raw_data,omitempty"`
    35â†’	ReleasedAt string `json:"released_at,omitempty"`
    36â†’	CreatedAt  string `json:"created_at"`
    37â†’}
    38â†’
    39â†’// GetReleasesOutput is the output for the get_releases tool.
    40â†’type GetReleasesOutput struct {
    41â†’	Releases []ReleaseItem `json:"releases"`
    42â†’	Total    int           `json:"total"`
    43â†’	Page     int           `json:"page"`
    44â†’	PerPage  int           `json:"per_page"`
    45â†’}
    46â†’
    47â†’// GetReleaseDetailInput is the input for the get_release_detail tool.
    48â†’type GetReleaseDetailInput struct {
    49â†’	ReleaseID string `json:"release_id"`
    50â†’}
    51â†’
    52â†’// GetReleaseDetailOutput is the output for the get_release_detail tool.
    53â†’type GetReleaseDetailOutput struct {
    54â†’	ID         string `json:"id"`
    55â†’	SourceID   string `json:"source_id"`
    56â†’	Version    string `json:"version"`
    57â†’	RawData    any    `json:"raw_data,omitempty"`
    58â†’	ReleasedAt string `json:"released_at,omitempty"`
    59â†’	CreatedAt  string `json:"created_at"`
    60â†’}
    61â†’
    62â†’// ListContextSourcesInput is the input for the list_context_sources tool.
    63â†’type ListContextSourcesInput struct {
    64â†’	Page    int `json:"page,omitempty"`
    65â†’	PerPage int `json:"per_page,omitempty"`
    66â†’}
    67â†’
    68â†’// ContextSourceItem is a single context source in the tool output.
    69â†’type ContextSourceItem struct {
    70â†’	ID     string `json:"id"`
    71â†’	Type   string `json:"type"`
    72â†’	Name   string `json:"name"`
    73â†’	Config any    `json:"config"`
    74â†’}
    75â†’
    76â†’// ListContextSourcesOutput is the output for the list_context_sources tool.
    77â†’type ListContextSourcesOutput struct {
    78â†’	Sources []ContextSourceItem `json:"sources"`
    79â†’	Total   int                 `json:"total"`
    80â†’	Page    int                 `json:"page"`
    81â†’	PerPage int                 `json:"per_page"`
    82â†’}
    83â†’
    84â†’// toolFactory holds the store reference and project ID scoped to a single
    85â†’// agent run. Tools created from this factory are bound to a specific project.
    86â†’type toolFactory struct {
    87â†’	store     AgentDataStore
    88â†’	projectID string
    89â†’}
    90â†’
    91â†’// NewTools creates the ADK function tools for agent research, scoped to a
    92â†’// specific project. The returned tools use the provided store to query
    93â†’// releases and context sources belonging to projectID.
    94â†’func NewTools(store AgentDataStore, projectID string) ([]tool.Tool, error) {
    95â†’	f := &toolFactory{store: store, projectID: projectID}
    96â†’
    97â†’	getReleases, err := functiontool.New(functiontool.Config{
    98â†’		Name:        "get_releases",
    99â†’		Description: "Fetch recent releases for the current project. Returns a paginated list of releases with version, raw data, and timestamps. Use this to understand what new versions have been published.",
   100â†’	}, f.getReleases)
   101â†’	if err != nil {
   102â†’		return nil, fmt.Errorf("create get_releases tool: %w", err)
   103â†’	}
   104â†’
   105â†’	getReleaseDetail, err := functiontool.New(functiontool.Config{
   106â†’		Name:        "get_release_detail",
   107â†’		Description: "Get detailed information for a specific release by its ID. Returns the full raw data payload from the upstream provider. Use this to inspect changelogs, commit lists, or other release metadata.",
   108â†’	}, f.getReleaseDetail)
   109â†’	if err != nil {
   110â†’		return nil, fmt.Errorf("create get_release_detail tool: %w", err)
   111â†’	}
   112â†’
   113â†’	listContextSources, err := functiontool.New(functiontool.Config{
   114â†’		Name:        "list_context_sources",
   115â†’		Description: "List the context sources configured for this project. Context sources are references to runbooks, documentation, monitoring dashboards, and other background materials that help you produce richer analysis.",
   116â†’	}, f.listContextSources)
   117â†’	if err != nil {
   118â†’		return nil, fmt.Errorf("create list_context_sources tool: %w", err)
   119â†’	}
   120â†’
   121â†’	return []tool.Tool{getReleases, getReleaseDetail, listContextSources}, nil
   122â†’}
   123â†’
   124â†’// getReleases is the handler for the get_releases tool.
   125â†’func (f *toolFactory) getReleases(ctx tool.Context, input GetReleasesInput) (GetReleasesOutput, error) {
   126â†’	page := input.Page
   127â†’	if page < 1 {
   128â†’		page = 1
   129â†’	}
   130â†’	perPage := input.PerPage
   131â†’	if perPage < 1 || perPage > 50 {
   132â†’		perPage = 20
   133â†’	}
   134â†’
   135â†’	releases, total, err := f.store.ListReleasesByProject(ctx, f.projectID, page, perPage)
   136â†’	if err != nil {
   137â†’		return GetReleasesOutput{}, fmt.Errorf("list releases: %w", err)
   138â†’	}
   139â†’
   140â†’	items := make([]ReleaseItem, 0, len(releases))
   141â†’	for _, r := range releases {
   142â†’		item := ReleaseItem{
   143â†’			ID:        r.ID,
   144â†’			SourceID:  r.SourceID,
   145â†’			Version:   r.Version,
